shared_properties: Prisoner silhouette stays inside 16x24 px with the cuffed wrists, cropped hair, and orange shoulder stripe preserved.
Core palette metadata remains primary #3B3347, background #0D0D0F, and accent #E07B39 while local highlight colors stay inside specs.colors_hex.
The contact pose still reads clearly after a horizontal flip so one authored sheet covers left and right wall braking.
differences: Two frames show one forearm and one boot pressed against the wall with the torso leaning 2 px into the contact surface.
Coat hem motion is reduced relative to free fall so the animation conveys braking drag rather than acceleration.
Dust abrasion pixels appear on the contact side using #8B2020 and #4E5940 in a 2 px wide streak.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the friction-control rule where player braking happens only during wall contact and directional input.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the stone-wall scrape mood, limited dust accents, and tight vertical shaft spacing around the braking silhouette.

reference_images.label: player_concept
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the prisoner body proportions so the contact pose remains readable without changing character identity.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteSheetBundle with TextureAtlasLayout and a fixed-step animation timer driven by Bevy 0.13 FixedUpdate.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load through AssetServer during a Loading state, register the atlas handle by task_id and asset_key, and keep animation metadata in a typed DescentActorVisual resource.
runtime_notes: Advance frames at the authored fps inside FixedUpdate so movement logic and animation cadence stay locked at 60 FPS.
Use flip_x for mirrored wall contact instead of authoring an extra right-facing sheet.
Snap the actor Transform to whole pixels after motion integration so the 16x24 silhouette stays crisp on the 4x upscale.

context.usage: Player visual used during active friction contact on either wall.

pose_notes: Leading hand plants at x1 or x14 depending on horizontal flip and occupies a 2x3 px contact block.
Contact boot bends inward by 1 px to imply downward drag instead of horizontal walking.
Torso rotates at most 8 degrees relative to the vertical axis to keep the no-jump control fantasy intact.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 32x24 px with 2 frames of 16x24 px and 0 px spacing.
After horizontal flip the pose still keeps one hand and one foot visibly aligned to a wall edge within a 2 px contact band.
Animation reads as braking rather than climbing and uses only the listed colors plus transparency.
